prompt: A hyper-realistic architectural photograph of a long narrow medieval stone corridor in extreme linear perspective, shot from ground level at one end. At the far end, a single ornate wrought-iron candelabra burns with intense warm golden-amber flame — the only light source. Seven identical frosted semi-transparent glass panels with thin iron frames hang from the ceiling at equal intervals along the corridor's length. Each panel receives dramatically less light than the one before it following exponential decay: the first panel nearest the candelabra glows rich amber, the second noticeably dimmer, each subsequent panel colder and darker, until the seventh panel nearest the camera is nearly indistinguishable from the dark walls — a barely visible shadow receiving almost zero light. Mood: cold, silent, austere. Far end warm and bright; near end almost total darkness. Long-exposure medium format photography, 35mm lens, f/8, high dynamic range. No people, no text, no labels, no diagrams, no symbols, no annotations. Negative: people, text, labels, equations, arrows, neon, sci-fi, uniform brightness.
metaphor: Seven panels each absorb most light; only a tiny fraction reaches the last, mirroring early neural layers receiving near-zero gradient signal.
